title: Magical Yeasty Peruvian Almond Empanadas
description: These energizing empanadas are made with almond flour and feature a unique binchotan-charcoal ingredient, perfect for a vegetarian snack or meal. The yeasty dough provides a light and airy texture.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the almond flour, yeast, binchotan-charcoal powder, and salt.
Gradually add the olive oil, sugar, and warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle and place a tablespoon of your favorite filling in the center.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with olive oil and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
